response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software engineer with a strong track record across web, mobile, and distributed systems, but his profile is almost entirely orthogonal to the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, SIMD/MIMD architecture, GPU workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His most relevant adjacent experience is writing low-latency services at Sociomantic and recent AI-agent tooling at Stripe, but these are software-layer concerns that do not translate to the silicon and systems-architecture depth this role demands.
strengths: 14+ years of software engineering experience with a consistent track record of delivering in high-scale, performance-sensitive environments (millions of HTTP req/s at Sociomantic)
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at Stripe, showing awareness of the AI tooling landscape
Demonstrated breadth across embedded systems, low-latency DLang applications, and distributed systems, suggesting systems-level thinking
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or any silicon-level design work — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in deep neural network training, inference optimization, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator workloads
Principal-level IC roles in hardware/architecture typically require graduate-level education (MS/PhD) or equivalent; candidate holds only a BS with no hardware specialization

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No AI/DL workload or GPU architecture expertise
Strong software engineering tenure and performance-aware systems work
